channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Tanzania collects TZS 1.327 trillion in state dividends to boost national development funding
item.description: Tanzania has recorded a strong financial performance after collecting 1.327 trillion shillings in dividends and statutory contributions from public institutions during the 2025/26 financial year. This...

item.title: UK expected to experience another heatwave as temperatures approach 30°C
item.description: Meteorologists are warning that a third heatwave in the United Kingdom is becoming increasingly likely over the coming days, with temperatures forecast to reach around 30°C in some regions. A strength...

item.title: Akhilesh Yadav criticises alleged egg attack on Mahua Moitra in West Bengal, calls for action
item.description: Samajwadi Party president Akhilesh Yadav has strongly condemned the alleged attack on Trinamool Congress MP Mahua Moitra in West Bengal, where BJP workers reportedly hurled eggs at her during a visit ...

item.title: DA leader Geordin Hill-Lewis outlines political and economic priorities in party address
item.description: DA leader Geordin Hill-Lewis delivered an address to party members and supporters focusing on what he described as a necessary shift in South Africa’s political and economic direction. In his remarks,...

item.title: Political commentator warns opposition 'pink' bloc against internal infighting ahead of 2028 elections
item.description: The article is an opinion piece that warns members of the Philippine opposition, often referred to as the “pink” movement, against self-destructive internal conflicts. The author argues that supporter...

item.title: Atiku calls for transparency on ₦501 billion power sector bond beneficiaries
item.description: The presidential candidate of the Nigeria Democratic Congress (NDC), Atiku Abubakar, has called on the federal government to make public the full list of beneficiaries of the ₦501 billion bond issued ...

item.title: UK government to issue formal apology for historical forced adoptions in England and Wales
item.description: The Prime Minister, Sir Keir Starmer, is expected to issue a formal apology on behalf of the British state for its involvement in historical forced adoptions in England and Wales. The announcement fol...

item.title: Durban July 2026 style guide: Country Allure fashion trends for race day
item.description: The S Mag style guide sets the tone for the upcoming Hollywoodbets Durban July at Greyville Racecourse in Durban, one of Mzansi’s most prominent social and fashion events. With the 2026 theme, ‘Countr...

item.title: Canada to debut in Eurovision Song Contest 2027 as first new participating country since 2015
item.description: Canada has officially been announced as a participant in the 2027 Eurovision Song Contest, marking a significant expansion of the long-running international music competition beyond Europe. The announ...

item.title: Simple Vanilla Cake with Apricot Jam and Coconut Topping Recipe
item.description: This article shares a straightforward and comforting home-baking recipe for a vanilla cake topped with apricot jam and desiccated coconut. It is designed to be easy to follow, using basic pantry ingre...

item.title: Uganda reports isolated Marburg virus case detected during surveillance, Africa CDC confirms
item.description: Health authorities in Uganda have confirmed an isolated case of Marburg virus disease, a serious and highly infectious haemorrhagic fever, according to Africa Centres for Disease Control and Preventio...

item.title: Calls Mount for Resignation of Youth PS Following Death of Former TUK Student in Nairobi Incident
item.description: University student leaders in Kenya have intensified calls for the resignation of Youth Affairs and Creative Economy Principal Secretary Fikirini Jacobs following the fatal shooting of Cecil Ouma, a f...

item.title: Man charged in Kitsilano Beach assault released with conditions after brief detention
item.description: A man arrested in connection with a violent attack on a woman at Kitsilano Beach in Vancouver has been released from custody after spending less than 48 hours in detention, according to Vancouver poli...

item.title: Witness account emerges in fatal shooting of youth leader linked to PS Fikirini’s convoy in Nairobi
item.description: Cecil Ouma, a political mobiliser, was allegedly shot dead during an incident involving a bodyguard attached to Youth Affairs Principal Secretary Fikirini Jacobs in Nairobi. According to witness accou...

item.title: OVP gets clean COA audit opinion for 2025, fourth straight year
item.description: The Office of the Vice President (OVP) has received an unmodified opinion from the Commission on Audit (COA) for calendar year 2025, marking the fourth consecutive year that the agency has obtained th...

item.title: 11-year-old Canadian boy dies from rabies after bat contact despite no visible bite marks
item.description: An 11-year-old boy in northern Ontario, Canada, died from rabies following contact with a bat during the summer of 2024, highlighting the severe risks posed by wildlife exposure even when no obvious b...

item.title: Andhra Pradesh to form task force and complaint cell to address social media abuse and online harassment
item.description: Andhra Pradesh Deputy Chief Minister K. Pawan Kalyan has announced that the state government will establish a dedicated task force to address the growing issue of social media abuse and online harassm...

item.title: Falana to defend suspect facing federal charges over alleged forged presidential appointment documents
item.description: Human rights lawyer, Femi Falana (SAN), is set to represent Prince Adeniyi Adeyemi in a criminal case filed by the Federal Government over allegations involving conspiracy, forgery, impersonation, and...

item.title: Dangote expands into energy, power, and fertiliser in Tanzania and Congo in $46bn growth plan
item.description: Africa’s richest man, Aliko Dangote, is making a major shift in his business strategy by expanding beyond the cement industry into new sectors, particularly in Tanzania and the Republic of the Congo. ...

item.title: Pakistan rejects India’s claims over cross-border strikes, calls statement baseless and misleading
item.description: Pakistan has strongly rejected India’s statement regarding Islamabad’s recent military actions targeting alleged terrorist infrastructure in Afghanistan, terming it baseless, misleading and politicall...
